title: Vellore Fort
tagline: Vellore Fort — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Vellore Fort is a large 16th-century fort situated in heart of the Vellore city, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India built by the Emperors of Vijayanagara.
overview.description: Vellore Fort is a large 16th-century fort situated in heart of the Vellore city, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India built by the Emperors of Vijayanagara. The fort was at one time the imperial capital of the Aravidu Dynasty of the Vijayanagara Empire. The fort is known for its grand ramparts, wide moat and robust masonry. The fort's ownership passed from Emperors of Vijayanagara, to the Bijapur sultans, to the Marathas, to the Carnatic Nawabs and finally to the British, who held the fort until India gained independence.

topPlaces.name: Jalakandeswarar Temple, Vellore
topPlaces.description: Jalakandeswarar Temple is a temple dedicated to Lord Shiva which is located in the Vellore Fort, in heart of the Vellore city, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: St. John's Church, Vellore
topPlaces.description: St. John’s Church is located inside the Vellore Fort, Tamil Nadu State, India.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Vellore
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Vellore (1646) was a military conflict fought on 4 April 1646 between the armies of the Vijayanagara Empire and the Bijapur Sultanate near Vellore. After advancing into the Carnatic and capturing several important forts, the Bijapur army under Mustafa Khan was met by the forces of Sriranga III outside Vellore.

topPlaces.name: Mettukulam
topPlaces.description: Mettukulam Village is situated in the fort city of Vellore, India. The village lies between Vellore (10 km) and Chitoor (26 km) and the temple towns of Thiruvannamalai and Tirupati.

topPlaces.name: Vellore
topPlaces.description: Vellore (Tamil: ['ʋeːluːɾ] WAY-loor), also spelled Velur, is a city and municipal corporation in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the administrative headquarters of Vellore district.

topPlaces.name: Ponnam Mangalam
topPlaces.description: Ponnammangalam is a village in Arcot taluk in Ranipet district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Shenbakkam
topPlaces.description: Shenbakkam is a small locality adjoining with Konavattam locality and also zone-4 headquarters of Vellore Municipal Corporation in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The Swayambu Vinayagar temple is located here.

topPlaces.name: Thorapadi, Vellore
topPlaces.description: Thorapadi is a part of Vellore Corporation. It is a prominent area in the city of Vellore.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vellore Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vellore Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vellore Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vellore Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vellore Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vellore Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Vellore Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vellore Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jalakandeswarar Temple, Vellore, St. John's Church, Vellore, Battle of Vellore, Mettukulam, Vellore.
